content.problemStatement:
Once, after a stressful day, Chef decided to relax and visit a casino near his house to gamble. He feels lucky and he's going to bet almost all of his money.

The game Chef is going to play in the casino consists of tossing a die with NNN faces twice. There is a number written on each face of the die (these numbers are not necessarily distinct). In order to win, Chef must get the number AAA on the first toss and the number BBB on the second toss of the die.

The excited viewers want to know the probability that Chef will win the game. Can you help them find that number? Assume that Chef gets each face of the die with the same probability on each toss and that tosses are mutually independent.
constraints: 1 ≤ xi ≤ N for each valid i
content.inputFormat: The first line contains a single integer T denoting the number of test cases. The first line of each test case contains three space-separated integers N, A, and B. The second line contains N space-separated integers x1, x2, …, xN denoting the numbers written on the faces of the die.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one real number — the probability that Chef will win. Your answer will be considered correct if its absolute error does not exceed 10^-6.

sampleTestCases.explanation: In the first test case, all faces of the die have the number 1, so the probability of getting 1 on both tosses is 1. In the second test case, only one face of the die has the number 1 and one face has the number 2, so the probability of getting 1 on the first toss and 1 on the second toss is 0.25.

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we need to calculate the probability of getting the number A on the first toss and the number B on the second toss of the die. We can do this by counting the number of faces with the number A and the number of faces with the number B, then dividing by the total number of faces (N) for each toss.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * N) — where T is the number of test cases and N is the number of faces on the die. We iterate over each test case and for each test case, we iterate over the numbers on the faces of the die.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) — we need to store the numbers on the faces of the die.

code.Python3:
import sys
T = int(input())
for _ in range(T):
    N, A, B = map(int, input().split())
    faces = list(map(int, input().split()))
    countA = faces.count(A)
    countB = faces.count(B)
    probability = (countA / N) * (countB / N)
    print(f"{probability:.10f}")
